title: Optical Density Measurement for Bacterial Solution
author: Priota Islam
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.vude6s6

steps.title: Pre-measurement Clean the disposable cuvettes with...
steps.instruction: Pre-measurement Clean the disposable cuvettes with ethanol and lint free paper Aliquot small amount of LB broth into a falcon tube (For Blank) Fill about 1ml of the LB broth into a clean cuvette Mix/Vortex the tube containing the stock bacteria to achieve a homogenized solution Take three aliquots from the same tube in three different cuvettes

steps.title: Actual measurement Switch on the spectrophotometer...
steps.instruction: Actual measurement Switch on the spectrophotometer Press the bottom left button to select the OD program Increase the wavelength to 600nm Put the blank cuvette first and press the button showing blank Put the sample cuvette 1 and press the button showing sample Take a note of the OD reading Place the second sample and repeat After taking readings for all three samples take the average Record the OD as OD600 = 1.35

steps.title: Post measurement Discard the cuvettes Close...
steps.instruction: Post measurement Discard the cuvettes Close the lid to the spectrophotometer Switch it OFF
